target.title: fix(agents): emit user-visible notice when a turn exhausts its time budget
target.body:
## Update â€” rebased onto current `main` (2026-06-25)

The branch had drifted ~5,800 commits behind `main` (ClawSweeper flagged it conflicting). It is now rebased; the 3-commit series applies on current `main`:

- `run.ts` and `run/incomplete-turn.ts` resolved cleanly â€” the `resolveTurnBudgetTimeoutNotice` signature is reconciled to the current `allowSilentReply` form, and the integration tests were re-seated inside the runner describe block.
- **Re-verified on current `main` (Node 22):** `run.incomplete-turn.test.ts` **244/244 passing** (the 3 turn-budget integration tests through the real `runEmbeddedAgent` terminal path + the 8-case `resolveTurnBudgetTimeoutNotice` decision matrix); **`tsgo:core` clean**; **`tsgo:test:src` clean**.

The committed fault-injection regression tests drive the **real** `runEmbeddedAgent` terminal path (not a mock of it). A live gateway/channel recording of a â‰Ą`timeoutSeconds` tool-execution timeout remains the one artifact reproducible only on a long-running real agent.

---

## Problem

When an agent turn exhausts its time budget (`agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`) or a run-level deadline **while no assistant reply has been produced**, the gateway can go completely silent â€” no message reaches the channel, no indication a limit was hit. From a user's seat this is indistinguishable from a hung process.

This is not the prompt/provider timeout case, which already surfaces *"Request timed out before a response was generatedâ€¦"*. It's the timeout that fires **outside a model prompt** â€” most commonly while a tool is executing on a complex turn:

- `attempt.timedOut` is `true`, but because `countActiveToolExecutions(...) > 0` the runner also sets `timedOutDuringToolExecution = true`;
- `timedOutDuringPrompt = timedOut && !timedOutDuringCompaction && !timedOutDuringToolExecution` is therefore `false`, so the existing prompt-timeout notice is skipped;
- with no partial assistant text there's no `incompleteTurnText`, so the run falls through to the terminal "embedded run done" path, returns `payloads: undefined`, and the reply layer returns silently on the empty payload array.

## Fix

Add one terminal branch in `runEmbeddedAgent` that surfaces a recoverable, user-visible notice when a turn timed out and is about to deliver nothing visible â€” mirroring the prompt-level timeout copy already emitted from the same function. Because the branch returns a non-empty payload, it flows through the normal gateway-agnostic reply path (Discord, Matrix, etc.) and never reaches the silent return.

The decision is a small pure helper, `resolveTurnBudgetTimeoutNotice(...)`, returning the notice only when correct and `null` otherwise. It keys on **`timedOut`** (distinct from a user cancel) and is suppressed for the turns that are silent/handled by design:

- `timedOutDuringCompaction` â€” compaction timeouts are **paused/resumable**, not abandoned; emitting a notice (passed as `incompleteTurnText`) would also override the `paused` liveness state the terminal path preserves;
- `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` (the **raw** flag â€” note the derived `shouldTreatEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` is always `false` for timed-out attempts, so it cannot protect this case) â€” cron/heartbeat/autonomous contexts;
- a messaging tool already delivered out-of-band (`hasMessagingToolDeliveryEvidence`);
- a deterministic approval prompt was delivered out-of-band (`didSendDeterministicApprovalPrompt`; payload building intentionally suppresses assistant artifacts there).

A separate copy variant covers an idle (no-output) model timeout. Net change is additive: it only produces output where there is currently none.

## Proof

**1. Committed real-`runEmbeddedAgent` regression tests** (`run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`) drive the real terminal path via the existing harness loader:

- tool-execution timeout, empty reply â†’ notice delivered, `livenessState: "abandoned"`;
- same, in a silent-reply context (`allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true`) â†’ **no notice** (stays silent);
- compaction timeout â†’ **no notice**, `livenessState: "paused"` preserved.

Plus full unit coverage of the helper's decision matrix. Suite: **123 passing**; `tsgo:core` and oxlint clean.

**2. Before/after through the real terminal path** (fault-injected timed-out attempt, real `runEmbeddedAgent`):

```
# tool-execution timeout, empty reply
BEFORE (origin/main):  result.payloads = undefined                 livenessState = blocked   â†’ silent
AFTER  (this PR):       result.payloads = [{ text: "âš ď¸Ź I hit my time budget on this request and
                        stopped before finishing. Ask me to continue, or simplify the request.
                        If this happens often, raise `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` in your
                        config.", isError: true }]                  livenessState = abandoned â†’ delivered

# compaction timeout, empty reply  (regression guard)
AFTER:                  result.payloads = undefined                 livenessState = paused    â†’ correctly silent
```

**On a live gateway/channel capture:** the trigger is a â‰Ą`agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` (default 600s) budget exhausted *during tool execution* â€” not reproducible against a real channel in CI or by an external contributor without a long-running live agent. The fault-injection harness drives the **real** `runEmbeddedAgent` terminal path (`makeAttemptResult({ timedOut: true, timedOutDuringToolExecution: true, assistantTexts: [] })`) and the committed tests assert end-to-end behavior through the same function; the harness itself is kept out of the committed diff to keep the patch narrow.
